For thermal neutron induced fission, 100 micro litre
of nitrate solution of 232U (~ 10 ug), 238Pu (~ 50 ug)
were put in polypropylene tubes and doubly sealed in
alkathene bags and irradiated in the reactor APSARA
for 3 to 30 min at a flux of 1.2x10**12 n/cm2/sec.
In case of fast neutron induced fission for long
lived fission products, known amount of 25 um thick
232Th metal foil(~50 mg) and electrodeposited targets
covered with 75 um thick lexan foil or 25 um thick
aluminium foil were used as samples which act as
catcher to collect the fission products during the
For short lived fission product ~10 mg of 232Th metal
foil, ~5 mg of 238U oxide and ~200 micro litre of
form of nitrate solutions were sealed in polypropy-
lene tubes were irradiated in the reactor CIRUS for
2 to 5 min at a flux 5.0x10**12 n/cm2/sec.

The irradiated solutions, lexan catcher foils and

The fission products were analyzed by gamma ray
spectrometry at a fixed geometry of an energy- and
germanium detector coupled to a PC-based 4K channel
The resolution of the detector system was 2.0 keV at
1332.0 keV and the dead time was always less than 10%.
From the photopeak areas of the gamma rays of the

The activities of 92Sr and 104Tc in unseparated
samples were used as fission rate monitor for long
lived and short lived fission products.
